mechanism_narrative: Sustained increases in industrial production reflect expanding manufacturing, boosting corporate earnings and driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: If INDPRO rises but the 20-day forward return is negative in more than 60% of instances, the hypothesis fails.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in the unemployment rate above 4.5% signals labour market weakening, reducing consumer spending and corporate earnings, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: If UNRATE exceeds 4.5% and the 60-day forward return is positive in more than half of such episodes, reject.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is above 4% and inflation is elevated above 320, the combined effect of tight monetary policy and high prices depresses equity valuations, producing negative 20-day returns.
regime_hint: tight monetary policy and high inflation
failure_condition: If the joint condition yields a 20-day return greater than zero in more than 40% of cases, the hypothesis fails.

mechanism_narrative: In a housing recession (starts below 1,300), further declines in housing starts amplify recession fears and depress investor sentiment, leading to negative short-term S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: If HOUST declines below 1300 but the forward 5-day return is positive in 50% or more of the occurrences, reject.

mechanism_narrative: Growth in real retail sales signals robust consumer demand, which drives corporate revenue and earnings, supporting positive S&P 500 returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: If RSAFS increases but the 60-day forward return is negative in more than half of the observations, the hypothesis fails.

mechanism_narrative: A large monthly decline in nonfarm payrolls (over 150,000) signals sudden labour market weakness, triggering earnings downgrades and negative S&P 500 returns over the following quarter.
failure_condition: If the monthly decline condition is met and the 60-day return is positive in more than a third of episodes, reject.

mechanism_narrative: When both retail sales and industrial production contract in the same month, it indicates a synchronous demand-supply slump that severely depresses economic activity and leads to negative S&P 500 returns within a month.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS and INDPRO both decline month-over-month
failure_condition: If the joint contraction condition is met but the 20-day return is positive in more than 30% of cases, the hypothesis fails.

mechanism_narrative: In a low-inflation environment (CPI below 300), further declines in the federal funds rate signal additional monetary easing, boosting equity valuations and producing positive S&P 500 returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: If the low-inflation regime is active and a FEDFUNDS decrease is followed by a negative quarterly return in more than 40% of cases, reject.
